Internal Incident Notice — Rimal Ports PJSC — Non-public inside information until ADX/SCA disclosure determination.
Issuer: Rimal Ports PJSC (Abu Dhabi Trade Licence CN-5827413), ADX ticker RMLP; ADGM subsidiary Ghadir Marine SPV Ltd (ADGM Comml Lic No. 00078412), with a DIFC branch for treasury ops.
On 2026-06-04 22:30 GST, unauthorized access via vendor VPN to the OT monitoring segment at Mina Al Saha terminal triggered a 3-hour preventive shutdown of 4 RTG cranes; no vessel incident occurred.
Initial forensic review (ticket it-ops-1329) indicates limited exfiltration of a contractor roster incl. names, work emails, handset numbers, and one Emirates ID; affected record: Ms. Hana Al Badri, Operations Supervisor, Emirates ID 784 - 1991 - 654 3213 - 6.
The roster file schema includes a family-origin field; that column was present but not populated in the extracted sample, and no payroll bank IBANs were stored.
Under PDPL we treat this as personal data exposure; notification to the UAE Data Office will be submitted within 72 hours, and data subjects informed via hr@rimalports.ae.
From an issuer perspective, we are in a closed period ahead of Q2 results (blackout window 15 Jun–18 Jul); selective disclosure is prohibited and an insider list has been opened per SCA/ADX rules.
Management preliminarily assesses a throughput impact of ≤1.5% for June; this may be material for RMLP depending on cumulative quarterly performance and therefore qualifies as inside information unless publicly announced.
IR should prepare the ADX immediate disclosure template for filing on T+1 if impact exceeds internal threshold; this communication is not an announcement and not for dissemination outside need-to-know.
Reference: our prior ADX clarification dated 2026-05-12 (RMLP/CL/2026/0512, https://adx.ae/ann?ref=RMLP%2F20 26%2F0512) related to a separate labour matter and remains accurate but is not relevant to this cyber event.
External noise: Al Khabra Systems LLC posted a general blog on “zero impact” for its clients; that is a public vendor claim and should not be treated as issuer disclosure.
Financing workflow note: we continue documentation for the senior sukuk tap via Nasdaq Dubai advisors; today’s event does not constitute a material adverse change under the draft terms and no covenant breach is expected.
Governance: restrict communications during blackout; do not brief analysts or suppliers on non-public operational details; investor queries to Mr. Kareem bin Taha (Head of Compliance), kareem.taha@rimalports.ae, +971 50 123  47  89.
Identification checks show the attacker used a disabled trade licence entry “Marina Gulf Tech FZ-LLC — TL 40 19  77” on our vendor portal; portal logs stored under s3://ri mal-ir/re d/2026-06-04 show auth token 0xA3**CD.
For ADGM-managed SPV contracts, the incident is operational only and does not affect DIFC treasury payments or the ADX share registry; product names “QuantaGate OT Sensor” and “Safeline PAM” are mentioned for inventory completeness.
Routine note: the Q1 sustainability report uploaded on 2026-04-28 to https://rimalports.ae/esg is public, and generic CSR statements therein should not be treated as updates on this incident.
